message:
chore(gitignore): cover beads rebuild + recovery snapshot artifacts

Six untracked .beads/beads.db.rebuild_<TS>(_label)?(-wal)? files were
showing in git status from concurrent codex_repair / cod_frankenredis
sessions. They are agent-generated SQLite rebuild snapshots, not work
to commit. Same shape as the existing .beads/*.bad_* / *.pre_*
ignores; just adds the rebuild_ + stale_ filename family alongside
top-level .beads.recovery_<TS>/ and .beads.snapshot_<TS>/ trees that
beads also drops at repo root.

Verified with git check-ignore against all six files in tree — each
now resolves to .beads/beads.db.rebuild_*. No tracked files affected
(only .beads/.gitignore, config.yaml, issues.jsonl, metadata.json
remain tracked under .beads/).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -159,6 +159,10 @@ ubs_*.jsonl
 .beads/recovery_*/
 .beads/*.bad_*
 .beads/*.pre_*
+.beads/beads.db.rebuild_*
+.beads/beads.db.stale_*
+.beads.recovery_*/
+.beads.snapshot_*/
 
 # Redis AOF (append-only file) runtime artifact — written by fr-runtime during
 # tests/sessions; not source, never committed.
